hypothesis: Lesson quality (Sharpe score) + domain tag explain residual citation variance after controlling for PA (degree) and proximity (session gap). Models: A=PA+proximity, B=+Sharpe, C=+Sharpe+Domain.

B_plus_sharpe.description: Adds Sharpe quality score as fitness covariate

C_plus_sharpe_domain.description: Adds domain tag (has-domain binary) as secondary fitness

key_findings: Sharpe fitness STRONG: ΔBIC=+75.0 vs PA+proximity baseline
Direction POSITIVE: β_s=0.256 — higher Sharpe lessons get MORE cited
Domain tag effect WEAK: ΔBIC=+2.77 (minimal marginal value over Sharpe alone)
Sharpe explains 4.9% of total joint LL gain (small but statistically clear)
interpretation: Lesson quality (Sharpe score) is a genuine citation attractor in the swarm knowledge graph, beyond degree-based preferential attachment and recency proximity. A 1-point Sharpe improvement multiplies citation probability by exp(0.256)=1.29 (29% boost per point). Domain membership adds negligible marginal predictive power once Sharpe is included.

falsification_conditions.would_be_falsified_by: ΔBIC < 10 would indicate weak/noise-level effect; β_s < 0 would invert the quality-citation relationship
falsification_conditions.not_measured: Causal direction (quality → citation vs citation → perceived quality); Sharpe coverage 29% may introduce selection bias
